title: (IT)OM Canvas
lead: The (IT)OM Canvas captures a complete Operating Model on a single A4 or whiteboard — a powerful tool to quickly discover, capture and communicate the essential design of an existing or new operating model.

sections.heading: The (IT)OM Canvas
sections.alt: The (IT)OM Canvas with its six building blocks: Suppliers (S), Locations (L), Processes (P), Organization (O), Information (I) and Management System (M)

sections.heading: What is the (IT)OM Canvas?
sections.body: The (IT)OM Canvas is the simplest representation of an Operating Model. It fits on a single A4, a whiteboard, or in a presentation and provides a clear high-level definition of the operating model before diving into lower-level detail. The canvas works especially well as an interactive tool in workshops with business and IT stakeholders.

items.desc: the end-to-end process flow (Value Delivery Chain) that creates and delivers value to customers — the core of the operating model
the organisational structure, roles, teams and governance that execute and manage the processes
the physical and/or virtual locations from which the organisation operates and delivers
the data, systems and information flows that support processes and decision-making
the suppliers and partners the organisation needs to deliver value — internal and external
the control and management mechanisms that steer and monitor the operating model as a whole

sections.heading: The (IT)OM Canvas within the Business Model Canvas
sections.alt: The Operating Model Canvas embedded within the Business Model Canvas — the operations (back end) form the left side of the Business Model Canvas

sections.heading: Relationship with the Business Model Canvas
sections.body: The Operating Model Canvas represents the operations side of the Business Model and fits seamlessly within the Business Model Canvas (BMC). The left half of the BMC — Key Partners, Key Activities, Key Resources and Cost Structure — maps directly onto the building blocks of the (IT)OM Canvas. This makes the canvas highly suited for conversations about how IT operations deliver business value.

items.desc: ITOM engagement kickoff — quickly create a shared picture of the current or desired operating model
Stakeholder workshops — interactively discover and capture the operating model with business and IT together
Target Operating Model — summarise the essence of the desired future model on a single page
Communication — make complex operating model designs accessible to a broad audience
Gap analysis — place the current canvas next to the desired canvas to identify transformation space
Canvas workshops — facilitated sessions to complete the (IT)OM Canvas together with stakeholders
As-is / To-be Canvas — map current and desired operating models side by side
Business Model Canvas integration — connect the operating model to the broader business strategy
